title: PRD-017: IDE and CI Integrations

overview.description: Integrate Jarvy with popular development workflows including IDE extensions, CI/CD actions, dev container features, and pre-commit hooks to provide seamless developer experience across the entire development lifecycle.
goals: Zero-friction IDE integration: detect missing tools and offer one-click installation
Native CI/CD support: GitHub Action for standardized CI setup
Dev container ready: automatic provisioning in containerized environments
Pre-commit validation: ensure tool availability before commits
Consistent experience: same jarvy.toml works across all integrations

userStories.title: VS Code Extension
userStories.description: As a developer using VS Code, I want the editor to detect when tools defined in jarvy.toml are missing so that I can install them without leaving my IDE.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Extension activates when jarvy.toml is present in workspace
Extension reads and parses jarvy.toml file
Extension checks which defined tools are missing from the system
Missing tools displayed in Problems panel with severity Warning
Quick Fix action 'Install with Jarvy' available for each missing tool
Quick Fix action 'Install All Missing Tools' available at file level
Status bar item shows tool status (e.g., 'Jarvy: 5/7 tools ready')
Command palette includes 'Jarvy: Install All Tools', 'Jarvy: Check Tools'
Extension watches jarvy.toml for changes and re-validates
Terminal integration: 'Jarvy: Open Setup Terminal' command
Extension respects VS Code's proxy settings for downloads

userStories.title: GitHub Action
userStories.description: As a DevOps engineer, I want a GitHub Action that provisions the CI environment from jarvy.toml so that my pipelines use the same tool configuration as local development.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Action available at uses: Cliftonz/jarvy-action@v1
Action installs Jarvy CLI if not present
Action runs jarvy setup with CI mode enabled
Action supports input parameter: config-path (default: jarvy.toml)
Action supports input parameter: tools (override specific tools)
Action supports input parameter: skip-tools
Action supports input parameter: cache (default: true)
Action sets up tool caching using @actions/cache
Action outputs installed tool versions as step outputs
Action integrates with GitHub's problem matchers for error reporting
Action supports matrix builds (different tool versions per job)
Action provides composite action for minimal overhead
Detailed logging with GitHub Actions grouping

userStories.title: Devcontainer Feature
userStories.description: As a developer using GitHub Codespaces or VS Code Dev Containers, I want Jarvy to automatically install my tools when the container starts so that my development environment is ready immediately.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Feature published to GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io)
Feature installable via devcontainer.json features config
Feature installs Jarvy CLI during container build
Feature supports option: version (Jarvy version to install)
Feature supports option: configPath (path to jarvy.toml)
Feature supports option: installOnCreate (run setup during creation)
Feature supports option: installOnAttach (run setup when attaching)
Feature adds lifecycle scripts for onCreate, postCreate, postAttach
Feature works with both Debian-based and Alpine-based images
Feature caches tool installations between rebuilds when possible
Feature logs clearly indicate installation progress

userStories.title: JetBrains Plugin (Lower Priority)
userStories.description: As a developer using IntelliJ IDEA, WebStorm, or other JetBrains IDEs, I want plugin support for jarvy.toml so that I have a consistent experience across IDEs.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Plugin published to JetBrains Marketplace
Plugin provides jarvy.toml syntax highlighting and schema validation
Plugin shows missing tools as inspections (warnings)
Quick-fix actions to install missing tools
Tool window showing tool status
Run configuration for jarvy setup
Plugin works across all IntelliJ-based IDEs

userStories.title: Pre-commit Hook Integration
userStories.description: As a developer using pre-commit framework, I want to validate that required tools are installed before committing so that I catch environment issues early.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Pre-commit hook available in pre-commit hooks repository
Hook validates tools defined in jarvy.toml are installed
Hook configuration supported in .pre-commit-config.yaml
Hook supports argument: --config (path to jarvy.toml)
Hook supports argument: --fail-on-missing (exit with error if tools missing)
Hook supports argument: --tools (check only specific tools)
Hook outputs clear message about which tools are missing
Hook provides actionable guidance (e.g., 'Run jarvy setup to install')
Hook runs quickly (< 2 seconds for typical configs)
Hook works on macOS, Linux, and Windows

userStories.title: CLI Enhancements for Integrations
userStories.description: As an integration developer, I want CLI commands that support machine-readable output and validation modes so that integrations can programmatically interact with Jarvy.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: jarvy get --check command exits with error if tools missing
jarvy get --format json outputs machine-readable tool status
jarvy get --tools flag filters to specific tools
CI mode correctly detected and used by all integrations

phases.title: Core CLI Enhancements

tasks.task: Add jarvy get --check command for pre-commit hook
tasks.note: covered by `jarvy validate --strict` — the pre-commit hook (Phase-5) invokes that instead of a get --check variant

tasks.task: Add --format json output for machine parsing
Ensure CI mode works correctly for all integrations

phases.note: CLI foundations complete via existing PRD-051 (--format json) + PRD-010 (--ci) work; pre-commit uses validate --strict.

phases.title: GitHub Action
phases.note: GitHub Action built + self-test green on ubuntu/macos/windows (feat/gh-action). Marketplace listing = maintainer.

phases.title: VS Code Extension

tasks.task: Create diagnostic provider for missing tools

phases.note: VS Code extension built, tsc --noEmit strict-clean (feat/vscode-extension). vsce publish = maintainer.

phases.title: Devcontainer Feature

tasks.task: Create feature structure following devcontainer spec

tasks.note: dist/devcontainer/jarvy/install.sh — delegates to canonical checksum-verified install.sh

phases.note: In-repo feature complete (structure + install.sh + lifecycle + README). Remaining: Codespaces smoke-test + `devcontainer features publish` to ghcr — maintainer action.

phases.title: Pre-commit Hook

tasks.note: usage documented in docs/ci-cd.md + the hooks file header

phases.note: Consumable pre-commit hook shipped. Framework smoke-test is a maintainer step but the entry is a plain `jarvy validate --strict` shell-out.

phases.title: JetBrains Plugin (Lower Priority)

tasks.task: Set up Gradle project with IntelliJ Platform SDK
Implement TOML inspection for missing tools

phases.note: JetBrains plugin built, ./gradlew buildPlugin green (compileKotlin + plugin.xml validate + zip), JDK 21 (feat/jetbrains-plugin). Marketplace publish = maintainer.

nonGoals: Vim/Neovim plugin (users can shell out to jarvy commands)

technicalConsiderations.details: Use @iarna/toml for parsing, vscode API for diagnostics, execa for command execution
Must work with Debian-based and Alpine-based images; follow devcontainer spec
Use IntelliJ Platform SDK with Kotlin; depend on TOML plugin
Must be fast (< 2 seconds); use jarvy get --check as entry point

successMetrics: VS Code extension installs: 1,000 in first month
GitHub Action usage: 500 workflows in first month
Devcontainer feature pulls: 200 in first month
Pre-commit hook adopters: 100 repos in first month
Issue reports (integration bugs): < 10 critical in first month

risks.mitigation: Pin VS Code version, monitor changelog

dependencies: VS Code Extension API (vscode npm package)
Devcontainer CLI and GitHub Container Registry
reconciled: 2026-07-08: ALL SIX PHASES now built in-repo. Phase-1 (CLI foundations) via PRD-051/010; Phase-2 GitHub Action (self-test green 3 OSes); Phase-3 VS Code extension (tsc clean); Phase-4 devcontainer feature; Phase-5 pre-commit hook; Phase-6 JetBrains plugin (buildPlugin green). Remaining across 2/3/4/6 is publishing to marketplaces/ghcr — maintainer actions, not code. The action self-test also surfaced + fixed 3 real install.sh bugs (tmp_dir unbound, GitHub rate-limit hard-fail, bash-3.2 array).
